# Forge - Complete Documentation

> Start building without the cleanup tax

## Overview

Forge is a generator that scaffolds minimal React starters with theme support, click interaction sounds, and pre-configured linters and formatters. The structure stays easy to own after the first commit. It supports multiple frameworks, UI primitive libraries, RTL configurations, and code quality tooling options.

## Installation

Forge is distributed as an npm package and runs via package manager executors. No global installation required.

### Package Manager Commands

```bash
# pnpm
pnpm dlx forge@latest generate --name my-app

# npm
npx forge@latest generate --name my-app

# yarn
yarn dlx forge@latest generate --name my-app

# bun
bunx --bun forge@latest generate --name my-app
```

## CLI Reference

### Main Command

```bash
forge generate [options]
```

### Required Options

- `--name <project-name>` - Name of the project to generate

### Framework Options

- `--framework next` - Generate Next.js starter (App Router)
- `--framework vite` - Generate Vite + React starter
- `--framework start` - Generate TanStack Start starter

### UI Primitive Options

- `--base base` - Use Base UI (unstyled accessible primitives)
- `--base radix` - Use Radix UI (unstyled accessible primitives)

### Direction Options

- `--rtl` - Enable RTL support with English + Arabic
- `--ltr` - Single language (English only)

### Code Quality Options

- `--code-quality oxlint-oxfmt` - Use Oxlint + Oxfmt (fast Rust-based tools)
- `--code-quality biome` - Use Biome (all-in-one formatter/linter)
- `--code-quality eslint-prettier` - Use ESLint + Prettier (traditional setup)

### Complete Example

```bash
npx forge@latest generate \
  --name my-app \
  --framework next \
  --base radix \
  --rtl \
  --code-quality biome
```

## Supported Combinations

Forge supports all combinations of the following:

**Frameworks (3):**
- Next.js 16.2+
- Vite 8+
- TanStack Start (latest)

**UI Primitives (2):**
- Base UI
- Radix UI

**Direction Modes (2):**
- RTL (English + Arabic with route-based locale handling)
- LTR (English only)

**Code Quality Tools (3):**
- Oxlint + Oxfmt
- Biome
- ESLint + Prettier

Total: 36 possible configurations (3 × 2 × 2 × 3)

## What Gets Generated

### All Starters Include

1. **Theme System**
   - Dark/light mode with next-themes
   - Persistent theme across refreshes
   - No flash of unstyled content
   - Theme toggle with keyboard shortcut (press 'd')

2. **Sound Feedback**
   - UI sound integration via soundcn
   - Theme switch sounds (switch-on/switch-off)
   - Click sounds ready to use
   - Easy to remove if not needed

3. **shadcn/ui Integration**
   - Pre-configured with Luma preset
   - Neutral color scheme
   - Geist font family
   - Components ready to add via `shadcn add`

4. **Provider Composition**
   - Theme provider
   - Tooltip provider (when needed)
   - Direction provider (RTL mode only)
   - Proper SSR handling

5. **Minimal Starter Page**
   - Small heading
   - Brief description with file references
   - Theme toggle
   - Language toggle (RTL mode only)
   - No demo-heavy sections

6. **Code Quality Tooling**
   - Configured formatter and linter
   - Package.json scripts ready
   - Framework-aware rules (when applicable)

7. **Metadata**
   - Basic title and description
   - Robots default index/follow
   - Open Graph and Twitter tags
   - Favicon

### Framework-Specific Features

**Next.js:**
- App Router with proper layout structure
- Route-based locale handling (`/en`, `/ar` for RTL mode)
- Metadata API integration
- Error boundaries (error.tsx, global-error.tsx, not-found.tsx)
- Proper async params/searchParams handling

**Vite:**
- React Router declarative mode (RTL only)
- Route-based locale handling (RTL mode)
- Document shell with proper lang/dir attributes
- Fast HMR and build times
- Optimized bundle configuration

**TanStack Start:**
- File-based routing
- Route-based locale handling (RTL mode)
- Server functions ready
- SSR with proper hydration
- Document shell with lang/dir from route

### RTL Mode Details

When `--rtl` is enabled:

1. **Route Structure**
   - `/` redirects to default locale
   - `/en` for English content
   - `/ar` for Arabic content (RTL direction)

2. **Runtime Direction**
   - Dynamic `lang` attribute on html element
   - Dynamic `dir` attribute (ltr/rtl)
   - Persisted locale across refreshes
   - Language switcher in UI

3. **Font Handling**
   - Geist for LTR content
   - Proper font switching for RTL (when needed)

4. **Portal Behavior**
   - Tooltips and popovers respect direction
   - Proper positioning in RTL mode

### LTR Mode Details

When `--ltr` is enabled:

1. **Single Language**
   - No locale routes
   - No language switcher
   - Static `lang="en"` and `dir="ltr"`
   - Simpler provider wiring

## Architecture

Forge uses a layered generator approach rather than maintaining multiple complete template folders.

### Layer 1: Scaffold Adapter

Leverages official framework tooling:
- Next.js: Uses `create-next-app` patterns
- Vite: Uses `create-vite` patterns
- TanStack Start: Uses TanStack Start init patterns

Relies on shadcn CLI for component scaffolding:
- `shadcn init` with preset configuration
- Handles framework/base/RTL scaffold complexity
- Generates proper components.json

### Layer 2: Framework Overlay

Applies framework-specific patches:
- Root layout and document shell
- Provider composition
- Metadata configuration
- Error boundaries
- Route structure (RTL mode)

### Layer 3: Feature Packs

Adds cross-cutting features:
- Theme system with sound
- RTL runtime handling
- Code quality tooling
- Starter page and README
- Favicon and branding

## Dependency Management

Forge normalizes generated app dependencies to current npm `latest` dist-tags before final install. This prevents scaffold drift from becoming product drift.

**Verified Compatible With:**
- TypeScript 6
- Next.js 16.2+
- Vite 8+
- React 19+
- TanStack Start (latest)

## Design Principles

1. **Minimal by Default**
   - Users can clean the starter in under a minute
   - No demo-heavy sections or marketing layouts
   - Only essential structure and wiring

2. **App Structure**
   - Proper provider composition
   - Theme system that works
   - Sound integration ready to use
   - Not just a "hello world" template

3. **Easy to Own**
   - Clear file organization
   - Explicit rather than magical
   - Easy to understand and modify
   - No hidden complexity

4. **Leverage Official Tooling**
   - Use framework CLIs where possible
   - Use shadcn for component scaffolding
   - Don't reinvent what already works

## Comparison with Other Starters

### vs create-next-app / create-vite

Forge adds:
- Pre-configured theme system
- Click interaction sounds
- shadcn/ui integration
- RTL support option
- Code quality tooling choice
- Minimal starter page

### vs shadcn Templates

Forge adds:
- Multiple framework support
- RTL runtime handling
- Code quality tooling options
- Sound integration
- Dependency freshness normalization

### vs Other Boilerplates

Forge is intentionally minimal:
- No authentication
- No database setup
- No API routes (beyond framework defaults)
- No deployment configuration
- No testing setup (unless framework includes it)

The goal is to provide structure and wiring, not a full application.

## Common Use Cases

### Starting a New Project

```bash
npx forge@latest generate \
  --name my-project \
  --framework next \
  --base radix \
  --ltr \
  --code-quality biome
```

### Building a Multilingual App

```bash
npx forge@latest generate \
  --name my-app \
  --framework next \
  --base base \
  --rtl \
  --code-quality eslint-prettier
```

### Trying Different Frameworks

```bash
# Next.js version
npx forge@latest generate --name my-app-next --framework next --base radix --ltr --code-quality biome

# Vite version
npx forge@latest generate --name my-app-vite --framework vite --base radix --ltr --code-quality biome

# TanStack Start version
npx forge@latest generate --name my-app-start --framework start --base radix --ltr --code-quality biome
```

### Comparing UI Primitives

```bash
# Base UI version
npx forge@latest generate --name my-app-base --framework next --base base --ltr --code-quality biome

# Radix UI version
npx forge@latest generate --name my-app-radix --framework next --base radix --ltr --code-quality biome
```

## After Generation

### Next Steps

1. Navigate to project directory
2. Install dependencies (if not auto-installed)
3. Run dev server
4. Clean the starter page
5. Add your first feature

### Development Commands

```bash
# Development server
npm run dev

# Type checking
npm run typecheck

# Linting
npm run lint

# Formatting
npm run format

# Build
npm run build
```

### Adding Components

Use shadcn CLI to add components:

```bash
npx shadcn@latest add button
npx shadcn@latest add dialog
npx shadcn@latest add form
```

### Customizing Theme

Edit `app/globals.css` (or equivalent) to customize:
- Color palette
- Border radius
- Font family
- Spacing scale

## Troubleshooting

### Package Manager Issues

If you encounter package manager conflicts:
- Forge normalizes to your selected package manager after generation
- Delete lockfiles and node_modules if switching package managers
- Use the same package manager throughout the project

### Yarn Specific

Forge-generated Yarn apps:
- Declare their own project boundary
- Prefer `nodeLinker: node-modules` for compatibility
- Include `packageManager` field in package.json

### Build Errors

If you encounter build errors after generation:
- Run `npm run typecheck` to see TypeScript errors
- Run `npm run lint` to see linting errors
- Check that all dependencies installed correctly
- Verify Node.js version compatibility

### RTL Issues

If RTL mode isn't working correctly:
- Check that locale routes are set up (`/en`, `/ar`)
- Verify `lang` and `dir` attributes on html element
- Check that direction provider is in root layout
- Ensure fonts support RTL characters (if using Arabic)

## Technical Details

### shadcn Preset

Forge uses the Luma preset with code `b1VlIwYS`:
- Base color: neutral
- Theme: neutral
- Chart color: neutral
- Heading/font: Geist
- Icon library: Lucide
- Radius: default
- Menu: default/solid

### File Structure

Generated projects follow this structure:

```
my-app/
├── app/                    # Next.js App Router (or equivalent)
│   ├── layout.tsx         # Root layout with providers
│   ├── page.tsx           # Starter page
│   ├── globals.css        # Global styles and theme
│   └── ...
├── components/
│   ├── ui/                # shadcn components
│   ├── theme-provider.tsx # Theme system
│   └── ...
├── lib/
│   └── utils.ts           # Utility functions
├── public/                # Static assets
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
├── components.json        # shadcn config
└── README.md
```

### Metadata Defaults

All generated apps include:
- Title derived from project name
- Description mentioning the framework
- Robots default index/follow
- Basic Open Graph tags
- Basic Twitter Card tags
- Favicon

### Sound Integration

Sound files are included via soundcn:
- `click-soft` for button clicks
- `switch-on` for light mode
- `switch-off` for dark mode

Sounds are:
- Centralized in sound engine
- High performance
- Easy to remove
- Optional to use

## Philosophy

Forge exists because most starters are either:
1. Too minimal (just "hello world")
2. Too complex (full application with auth, DB, etc.)

Forge aims for the middle ground:
- App structure and wiring
- Minimal starter content
- Easy to understand and own
- Professional quality

The goal is to generate starters that stay easy to own after the first commit.

## Links

- Website: https://forgedx.vercel.app
- Concise docs: https://forgedx.vercel.app/llms.txt

## Version

This documentation reflects Forge as of April 2026.
